items.prompt: Complete a bounded task for this node and show the governing mathematical structure clearly.
items.expected_evidence: identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve a second task for the same node using a different representation or entry point.
items.expected_evidence: supply valid reasons for bounded geometric statements and theorem uses
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates supply valid reasons for bounded geometric statements and theorem uses with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Complete a bounded task for this node and show the governing mathematical structure clearly.
items.expected_evidence: complete or critique short geometric arguments with coherent claim-evidence flow
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates complete or critique short geometric arguments with coherent claim-evidence flow with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why the structure used in this node is mathematically valid.
items.expected_evidence: identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed.

items.prompt: Explain why the structure used in this node is mathematically valid.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer response and identify the earliest structural mistake. Focus on reason claim mismatch.
items.expected_evidence: identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed.

items.prompt: On a delayed recheck, complete a fresh task for this node and justify why the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify the structure of a bounded proof situation and the intermediate claims needed on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Complete a prerequisite probe on congruence and similarity via transformations and determine whether the learner’s error is upstream or belongs to proof structure and geometric argument.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether congruence and similarity via transformations is secure enough to support proof structure and geometric argument
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in congruence and similarity via transformations from a current-node issue in proof structure and geometric argument.

items.prompt: Complete a prerequisite probe on distributive reasoning and combining like terms and determine whether the learner’s error is upstream or belongs to proof structure and geometric argument.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether distributive reasoning and combining like terms is secure enough to support proof structure and geometric argument
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in distributive reasoning and combining like terms from a current-node issue in proof structure and geometric argument.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: distributive reasoning and combining like terms
